originalTitle: World's oldest leader to get a deputy for first time in 43-year rule
title: Guess What? Africa's Oldest Leader is Getting a Helper!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends! Your kid-friendly news reporter for Africa is here! Guess what amazing news we have from our big African family today? It’s about a very, very important person from a country called Cameroon. Cameroon is a beautiful place right here in Africa!

Meet President Paul Biya!

Imagine someone who has been in charge of a whole country for a super, super long time. That’s President Paul Biya! He is the President of Cameroon. A president is like the head of a very, very big family – the family of a whole country! President Biya is 93 years old. Wow! Can you believe it? That’s probably older than your grandparents! He is actually the oldest leader in the whole wide world! Isn't that amazing? Did you know? A president's job is to make sure everyone in their country is safe and happy, and that things like schools and hospitals work well.

Cameroon: A Beautiful African Home

Cameroon is a wonderful country in West Africa. It has sunny beaches, tall mountains, and even thick forests where many amazing animals live. It's sometimes called 'Africa in miniature' because it has so many different kinds of places, just like all of Africa! Did you know? In Cameroon, people speak French and English, and many other languages too! How cool is that?

A Very, Very Long Time in Charge!

President Biya has been the leader of Cameroon for 43 years. Can you imagine 43 years? That’s a longer time than your mom and dad have probably been alive! It’s like being a tiny baby and then growing up, going to school, becoming a grown-up, and then becoming an adult *again*, many times over! He started being president when many of your parents were just little kids, or maybe not even born yet! That's a super long time to lead a whole country all by yourself, right?

Big News: He's Getting a Helper!

Now, here’s the really exciting part of our news today! After leading Cameroon for 43 long years, President Biya is finally going to get a special helper. This helper is called a 'deputy'. Can you say 'deputy'? It means someone who helps the main person. Imagine your teacher in school. Sometimes, the teacher might have a classroom assistant, right? That assistant helps the teacher with different things, like handing out papers or helping students. That's a bit like what a deputy does for a president! A deputy is like a right-hand person. They help with all the important jobs that a president has to do every single day. This is the very first time in all of his 43 years as president that he will have a deputy! Isn't that something? Did you know? Having a deputy means the president can share his many important jobs, which can make things run even smoother for the country.

Why is a Deputy Important?

Leading a country is a very, very big job. There are so many things to think about! Things like making sure children go to school, making sure everyone has enough food, and building good roads. It’s like being the captain of a very big ship. One person can't do everything alone, can they? So, having a deputy means there's another smart person to help make decisions and do important work. The deputy can learn from the president and understand how to lead the country in the future too. It's good to have someone who can step in and help when needed, or even take over if the president is busy or needs a rest.

How Does a President Get Chosen? (Simple Talk)

Leaders like President Biya are chosen by the people of their country in something called an 'election'. In an election, grown-ups get to vote. Voting is like choosing who you think would be the best person to lead. President Biya was chosen by the people of Cameroon many times, including just last year. Sometimes, when there's an election, not everyone agrees, and that's okay. But the people chose him to be their president again. He has won many times, which shows he has been a very constant leader for his country.

A New Chapter for Cameroon

So, getting a deputy is a really big step for Cameroon and President Biya. It means that things might be a little different now, with more help at the top. It's like getting a new teammate on your sports team – more hands to help make the team better! This is a moment where we see a very experienced leader getting extra support after a very long time. It shows that even the oldest and most experienced leaders can welcome new helpers! Did you know? Many countries around the world have leaders who work with deputies or vice-presidents to help them.

Let's Celebrate Our African Leaders!

Isn't it amazing to learn about different leaders and countries in our big African family? We learn that even big, important jobs can be shared, and that helping each other is always a good idea. So, let's cheer for President Biya and his new deputy, and for all the people of Cameroon! Your kid-friendly news reporter is signing off! See you next time for more amazing news from Africa!
summary: President Paul Biya of Cameroon, who is the world's oldest leader and has been in charge for 43 years, is getting a special helper called a deputy for the very first time.
whyMatters: This is important because leading a country is a huge job, and getting a helper (a deputy) after 43 years means President Biya will have more support to make Cameroon even better for everyone.

content: The fuel cost is nearly double what the government budgeted for putting pressure on stretched finances.

originalTitle: Iran says Iraqi ships can pass Strait of Hormuz as transits tick up
title: Good News for Ships on the Ocean Road!
content:
Hello, African Friends!

Hello everyone! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you exciting updates from around the world. I hope you are having a super day! Today, we have some happy news about big ships and friendly countries. Imagine a world where everyone helps each other. That's what we're talking about today! We all know how important it is for countries to be good neighbours, just like you are good friends with your classmates. When countries are friends, many good things can happen.

Big News About Ships and Water Roads

There’s a special water road far away, called the Strait of Hormuz. It's like a very important highway for ships! Big news has come from a country called Iran. They have said that ships from another country, called Iraq, can travel safely through this special water road. This is wonderful news! This means that big ships carrying important things from Iraq can now go through this water road without any problems. It’s like getting a 'green light' to travel! Iran is very happy to help their friends in Iraq. They want to make sure that everyone can use this important water path easily and safely. This shows that countries can be good friends and help each other.

Imagine a Water Road

Have you ever seen a big, big road where cars drive? Well, the ocean has roads too, but they are made of water! These water roads are very important for ships. Imagine a narrow path of water that connects two very big parts of the ocean. This narrow path is called a 'strait'. The Strait of Hormuz is one of the most famous and busiest water roads in the whole world! Ships from many different countries use this strait every single day. It's like a giant roundabout for all the ships that want to sail to different parts of the world.

Why are Ships Important?

Why do we even care about ships, you might ask? Well, ships are super important! They are like giant floating trucks and buses. These amazing ships carry almost everything we use in our daily lives. Think about your toys, your clothes, the food you eat, or even the cars your parents drive! Many of these things travel across the big, blue oceans on huge ships before they get to your home in Africa or anywhere else. Without ships, we wouldn't have many of the things we love and need. **Did you know?** Some cargo ships are so big they can carry thousands of containers, each filled with things like bananas, shoes, or even parts for building new houses!

Friends Helping Friends

The news about Iran welcoming Iraqi ships is a story about friendship and helping each other. When countries are friendly, they make it easier for everyone to get what they need. Iran said that Iraq will not face any difficulties when their ships want to pass through the Strait of Hormuz. This is a very kind and friendly message. It’s like saying, "Come on through, friends! We are happy to see you and help you get where you need to go." This helps make the world a more peaceful place for everyone. When countries work together, it helps their people too. It means goods can move faster, and everyone can buy and sell things more easily. This makes life better for many people.

What is the Strait of Hormuz?

The Strait of Hormuz is a very special and narrow stretch of water. It's located between two countries: Iran and Oman. It's like the main entrance and exit for a very important sea called the Persian Gulf. Many countries around this gulf need to use this strait to send their goods out to the rest of the world. It's very busy because lots of big ships carrying oil and other important things have to go through this narrow path to get to the big oceans. **Did you know?** The Strait of Hormuz is only about 39 kilometers (about 24 miles) wide at its narrowest point. That's like driving for less than half an hour in a car!

Keeping Our Oceans Safe

When countries agree on how to use these important water roads, it makes the oceans safer for everyone. Imagine a busy road with no rules – it would be chaos! So, having agreements like this one between Iran and Iraq helps keep things orderly and safe for all the ships sailing the big blue seas. This also means that things like oil, which powers our cars and helps generate electricity, can keep moving safely. This is important for countries everywhere, including here in Africa! **Did you know?** Oceans cover more than 70% of our planet! They are home to amazing creatures and are like giant highways for ships.

A Happy Message for Everyone

So, this news is a happy message. It tells us that countries can be good neighbours and help each other with important things like shipping and trade. When ships can travel safely and freely, it helps bring all sorts of good things to people all over the world, even to our homes here in Africa. It reminds us that even though countries might be far apart, they are all connected by the big oceans and by the need to work together. Let’s all hope for more news about friendship and cooperation around the world! That’s all for today’s happy news. See you next time, friends!
summary: Iran has announced that ships from Iraq can safely and freely pass through the important water road called the Strait of Hormuz, showing friendship and helping ensure trade continues smoothly.
whyMatters: This is important because the Strait of Hormuz is a busy water path for ships carrying things like oil and goods we use every day, and when countries agree to let ships pass safely, it helps everyone get what they need and keeps global trade flowing peacefully.

originalTitle: Liverpool's FA Cup capitulation ramps up pressure on Slot
title: Liverpool's FA Cup capitulation ramps up pressure on Slot
content: Nobody would have believed that 12 months on from Liverpool's title win, a fanbase so united behind its team and its manager would be, at best, apathetic as to whether Arne Slot remains in the job.
